content:
Matteo Capucci said:

Nathanael Arkor said:

Matteo Capucci: I deliberately created this topic in #general rather than #practice: software, because a general purpose commutative diagram editor is likely useful to the majority of the people on the server, whereas the software usually discussed in #practice: software is of much more limited interest.

Alright, feel free to move it back! I was just tidying up since I see #general is quite... abused? It's not a capital crime but we have streams for a reason :laughing:

Yes, I agree that #general is a bit overused.
I've been putting random math stuff in "general" because I didn't see anything like "random" or "other".
What does "general" mean, anyway? What should be in "general"?

John Baez said:

I've been putting random math stuff in "general" because I didn't see anything like "random" or "other".

I don't think is wrong, it's just tickling my 'OCD'.
Shall we have a random channel though? Then what do we do with general? :laughing:
This topic was moved here from #practice: software > discussion: quiver by Matteo Capucci

@_Notification Bot|100006 said:

This topic was moved here from #practice: software > discussion: quiver by Matteo Capucci

because why not
There is already https://categorytheory.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/229451-general.3A-off-topic but it is common to have a #random :thinking:
And I always like having a #gripes :blush:
And #appreciation
Okay. I should be posting my random math crud in #general: off-topic.
Yeah I guess some things are not off-topic but are just... random! Like, idk, John relaying PTJ interview
Are the prefixes necessary, e.g. "prefix: some topic"?
I think #learning: show and tell was originally intended for some of the things currently being written in #general.

Eric Forgy said:

Are the prefixes necessary, e.g. "prefix: some topic"?

I guess not? Just expected if something evidently falls under something else
I would put random under general though, so #general: random
Has anyone said they are unhappy about the quantity and/or quality of things being posted on general?
Anyway, I'm now using #general: off-topic.

It's possible this will dramatically reduce the readership of my random crap. And that would make some people happier, and others less happy. So if anyone wants to read that stuff, please subscribe to #general: off-topic.
By the way, now that I'm looking at the streams I'm getting annoyed that there's an entire stream #evil compositions. Shouldn't this be just a topic in some stream?
Ditto for #Is this structure known?
I agree with your last two point John
You should create "general: John Baez". I would subscribe :blush:

John Baez said:

It's possible this will dramatically reduce the readership of my random crap. And that would make some people happier, and others less happy. So if anyone wants to read that stuff, please subscribe to #general: off-topic.

I personally have no problem with it

Jules Hedges said:

Has anyone said they are unhappy about the quantity and/or quality of things being posted on general?

I don't think so, this is all bikeshedding :shrug:
@Nathanael Arkor moved all the conversation from #Is this structure known? to somewhere else, but the stream still exists! So I will delete that stream.

Eric Forgy said:

You should create "general: John Baez". I would subscribe :blush:

"General John Baez" makes me sound like a Latin American strongman.

Presidente Baez

John Baez said:

By the way, now that I'm looking at the streams I'm getting annoyed that there's an entire stream #evil compositions. Shouldn't this be just a topic in some stream?

Anyone can create new streams, so new users occasionally do this by mistake. We can delete them if that hasn't already been done.

Matteo Capucci said:

Eric Forgy said:

Are the prefixes necessary, e.g. "prefix: some topic"?

I guess not? Just expected if something evidently falls under something else

Since Zulip arranges the streams alphabetically, I find it a helpful organizational thing for the streams to have prefixes. That's my experience as a casual Zuliper, at least.
Done.
